title: A Comparative Analysis of Relational Database Access-Layer Performance in Express.js for PostgreSQL and MySQL
description: Replication package (benchmark harness + paper sources + raw results) for a neutral comparison of relational database access layers in an Express.js service: native drivers (pg, mysql2) with tuned prepared-statement baselines (pg-tuned, mysql2-tuned), a query builder (Knex), and six ORMs (Drizzle, Prisma, Sequelize, TypeORM, Objection, MikroORM), across PostgreSQL 18.4 and MySQL 9.7.1, over five access patterns (point read, keyset range scan, deep/nested fetch, aggregation, insert), reporting throughput (req/s) and tail latency (p50/p90/p97.5/p99) jointly. Version 1.1 (harness 2.0) protocol: 25 independent replicates per cell (fresh server process per replicate, randomized cell order verified by an order-invariance A/B check, paired PRNG-seeded request streams, 12 s runs after an evidence-based 15 s warm-up), connection pool fixed at 10; byte-identical response verification across all layers and engines before measurement (TZ=UTC, canonical constructors); writes measured under each engine's DEFAULT durability from a physically rebuilt database state, with a symmetric relaxed-durability secondary; process-tree CPU accounting with separate database and generator CPU; GC and connection-pool observability. Controls and evidence: same-SQL deep-fetch control with server-side statement capture and per-layer wire-protocol disclosure plus EXPLAIN plans, coordinated-omission-corrected open-loop (constant-arrival) tail validation, deep-fetch fan-out sweep (0-500 comments), equal-CPU-budget (taskset) control, ten-minute sustained-load drift check, connection-scaling sweep (1-256), and a no-database Express baseline. One-command re-run against containerized (digest-pinned) or local engines.

creators.name: Miotk, Mateusz
creators.affiliation: Faculty of Mathematics, Physics and Informatics, University of Gdańsk
